thesis: Pre-revenue company pioneering sulfide-based solid-state battery technology for EVs. Asset-light model: sells electrolyte material and licenses cell designs to OEMs (BMW, Ford) rather than building gigafactories. $370M+ liquidity provides ~5–6 years of runway. Commercial revenue expected 2027–2028. Binary outcome: breakthrough adoption or cash-burn failure.

financials.revenue_source: Government grants, joint development agreements, electrolyte samples

business_model.electrolyte_sales: Sell proprietary sulfide solid electrolyte to cell manufacturers
business_model.technology_licensing: License battery cell designs and manufacturing processes to OEMs

drivers: Successful pilot line production and OEM qualification
Solid-state batteries become EV industry standard
Valuation multiple expansion as commercialization nears

risks: Manufacturing challenges prove insurmountable at scale
QuantumScape or Toyota reach market first
Traditional lithium-ion improves faster than expected
Cash burn accelerates; further dilutive raises needed
Loss of key BMW or Ford partnership

base_case.assumptions: Electrolyte sales begin 2027–2028; 2–3 major licensing deals secured; moderate 5–10% market share of solid-state market; path to profitability by 2030–2031.

catalysts.event: Q1 2026 Earnings — cash burn and timeline update

key_risks: Technology fails to scale from lab to manufacturing
Competitive displacement by QuantumScape, Toyota, or improved lithium-ion
